summary: Test-only change. No production code modified. No new secrets, credentials, or sensitive payloads introduced. Synthetic tar fixtures are built in-memory from constant-bounded payloads (len(payload) used for tar.Header.Size — no integer overflow path, no untrusted input). The pre-existing /tmp/pgcenter-record-testing.stat.tar path in Test_app_record was already in the baseline; +2 formula update does not change file-system behavior. No outbound network calls, no shell exec, no file-permission changes. The new TestFilterViews_dropsExplicitNotRecordable also runs entirely in-process.

owaspChecks.A01_broken_access_control: N/A — test code, no auth surface
owaspChecks.A03_injection: PASS — JSON marshaling uses encoding/json on typed structs; no string concatenation into queries or tar headers; constant filenames
owaspChecks.A05_security_misconfiguration: PASS — tar header Mode set to 0644 (read-only), no overly permissive defaults
owaspChecks.A08_software_data_integrity: PASS — tar payloads constructed from typed struct marshaling; no untrusted deserialization
owaspChecks.A09_logging_failures: N/A — test output only via t.Log indirectly

secretsScan.pii: none — synthetic test data only (pid=1234, datname=postgres, query='SELECT 1')
